verses.text: Rehoboam gathers an army to recover Israel, but God warns through Shemaiah, 'this thing is done of me'; and they obey and turn back. They submit to God's word against their own will. True obedience halts even a righteous-seeming war when God says no.

Rehoboam gathers an army to recover Israel, but God warns through Shemaiah, 'this thing is done of me'; and they obey and turn back. They submit to God's word against their own will. True obedience halts even a righteous-seeming war when God says no.

Rehoboam fortifies cities throughout Judah, building strongholds and setting captains over them. He secures what is left to him. When God has narrowed our portion, wisdom strengthens and tends the ground that remains rather than grasping after what is lost.

Rehoboam fortifies cities throughout Judah, building strongholds and setting captains over them. He secures what is left to him. When God has narrowed our portion, wisdom strengthens and tends the ground that remains rather than grasping after what is lost.

He provisions the fortresses with captains, shields, and spears, and stores of food, holding Judah and Benjamin. Diligent preparation guards the kingdom. God's providence works through, not instead of, the faithful ordering of what we have.

He provisions the fortresses with captains, shields, and spears, and stores of food, holding Judah and Benjamin. Diligent preparation guards the kingdom. God's providence works through, not instead of, the faithful ordering of what we have.

The priests and Levites leave their lands across Israel and resort to Rehoboam, because Jeroboam cast them off and made his own priests for the calves he set up. False worship drives out God's ministers. Where truth is despised, the faithful gather where God is honoured.

The priests and Levites leave their lands across Israel and resort to Rehoboam, because Jeroboam cast them off and made his own priests for the calves he set up. False worship drives out God's ministers. Where truth is despised, the faithful gather where God is honoured.

The priests and Levites leave their lands across Israel and resort to Rehoboam, because Jeroboam cast them off and made his own priests for the calves he set up. False worship drives out God's ministers. Where truth is despised, the faithful gather where God is honoured.

Those from every tribe who set their hearts to seek the LORD come to Jerusalem to sacrifice, strengthening Judah for three years as they walk in the way of David. Devotion gathers the devout. A faithful remnant becomes the strength of a kingdom.

Those from every tribe who set their hearts to seek the LORD come to Jerusalem to sacrifice, strengthening Judah for three years as they walk in the way of David. Devotion gathers the devout. A faithful remnant becomes the strength of a kingdom.

Rehoboam takes many wives and loves Maachah above the rest, fathering a large household. He repeats his father's snare of many wives. Even blessing misused becomes weakness; a divided heart at home breeds trouble for the throne.

Rehoboam wisely sets Abijah as chief and disperses his other sons through the fortified cities with provisions. He governs his house with foresight. Prudence in small arrangements is part of the faithfulness God gives to those who rule.
